title: Resnik — Managerial Judges (1982)
description: Judith Resnik — Managerial Judges (96 Harv. L. Rev. 374 (1982))
show_notes:
Judith Resnik — Managerial Judges (96 Harv. L. Rev. 374 (1982))

Judith Resnik's foundational 1982 article documents how American trial judges moved beyond their traditional adjudicative role to become active managers of litigation — supervising pretrial preparation, pressing settlement, and overseeing decrees after judgment. She argues this managerial turn, conducted largely informally and off the record, spread with little scrutiny of its costs to judicial impartiality, due process, and public confidence in the courts.
